folder.account: Top-level folder representing an account workspace. Contains all projects, users, and resources belonging to an organization.
folder.users: Folder that manages user accounts and groups within the platform.
folder.project: A project folder that organizes all workflows, operators, connections, and other resources for a specific initiative.
folder.workflow: A workflow folder that groups related tasks together into a pipeline. Supports shared configs, dependency management, and scheduling.
folder.operator: A folder containing operator definitions — the reusable code that defines HOW tasks execute against external systems.
folder.action: A folder containing reusable action definitions — lifecycle hooks that run at specific points (before, after, or on SLA breach) of a task.
folder.connection: A folder storing connection definitions — credentials and configuration for accessing external systems like AWS, Azure, or GCP.
folder.payload: A folder storing reusable payload definitions — the specific parameters and data passed to an operator during task execution.
folder.config: A folder containing configuration definitions that control task defaults, retry logic, parameters, and lifecycle behavior across workflows.
folder.asset: A folder for storing shared assets and resources used across the platform.
folder.trash: Holds deleted items that have been soft-deleted and are pending permanent removal.
folder.ai: A folder containing AI-related items including chat connections for LLM invocation and skills that guide AI code generation.
folder.chat: A folder containing AI chat connection definitions — the codeblock and credentials used to invoke LLM providers.
folder.skill: A folder containing reusable AI skills that can be referenced during AI-assisted code generation.
folder.report: A folder for storing generated reports and output artifacts from task executions.
folder.table: A folder for storing structured table definitions used in data pipeline workflows.
task: An executable unit of work that combines an Operator + Connection + Payload + Config. Can run ad-hoc or on a cron schedule, and supports lifecycle actions and dependency management.
action: A reusable lifecycle hook (e.g. preAction, postAction, createAction) that runs at specific points in a task's lifecycle — used for dependency checks, notifications, reruns, and more.
connection: Defines WHERE and HOW to connect to an external system. Stores credentials, region, and resource limits like max parallelism. Must be type-compatible with its paired operator.
config: Defines the RULES for task execution — retry counts, timeouts, default actions, and parameters. Supports a three-tier hierarchy: workflow → task → inline.
operator: Defines the HOW of task execution — the code logic for interacting with an external system (e.g. invoke Lambda, run SQL). Contains 4 methods: initialize, run, checkCompletion, and finish.
payload: Defines the WHAT of a task — the specific parameters passed to the operator at runtime (e.g. which Lambda function to invoke, what SQL to run). Supports dynamic variables like {{ds}}.
html_report: A rendered HTML report generated from task output or pipeline results, viewable directly in the UI.
usecase: A real-world scenario or example that demonstrates how different components work together to achieve a specific goal.
table: A structured data table definition used within pipelines for storing or referencing tabular data.
agent: An AI chat connection item that defines how to invoke an LLM provider. Contains a codeblock with the invocation logic, bashblock for dependencies, and connection credentials (API key, model, token limit).
generate: A conversational AI agent item that supports MCP tool-calling. Contains a codeblock with the invocation logic, bashblock for dependencies, and connection credentials (API key, model, token limit).
skill: A reusable instruction set that teaches the AI assistant how to perform a specific task or follow best practices. Referenced during AI generation to guide code generation.
